patterns.name: Singleton
patterns.description: Ensures a class has only one instance. Common for configuration, logging, or connection pools.

patterns.name: Factory
patterns.description: Creates objects without specifying exact class. Enables loose coupling and extensibility.

patterns.name: Observer/Event
patterns.description: Defines a subscription mechanism for event-driven architecture. Great for decoupling.

patterns.name: Higher-Order Component
patterns.description: Functions that take a component and return an enhanced component.

patterns.name: Context Provider
patterns.description: React Context for global state. Alternative to prop drilling.

patterns.name: Modules
patterns.description: VBA Modules for reusable code and business logic.

patterns.name: God Object
patterns.description: Files with too many responsibilities (15+ functions). Consider splitting into smaller modules.

patterns.name: Long File
patterns.description: Files over 500 lines are harder to maintain. Consider breaking into smaller modules.

patterns.name: VBA God Module
patterns.description: VBA modules with 20+ procedures. Consider splitting into smaller modules.
